Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 123. (no title) (To the Vizier) (ABL 0235)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[a]-⸢na⸣ LÚv.SUKKAL EN-ia

(1) To the Vizier, my lord: [your servant] Aššur-bani. [Good health] to the Vizier, my lord.

o 22

[ARAD-ka] maš-šur—ba-ni

o 33

[lu DI-mu a]-na LÚv.SUKKAL ⸢EN⸣-ia

o 44

[TAv É.GAL] ⸢i*⸣-sa-pa-ru-u-ni

(4) They have written to me [from the Palace]: "[......] Ziziru [... have ch]ased him [......] Calah [......] the moat [......] 120 [......

o 55

[ma-a x x x m]zi-zi-ru

o 66

[x x uk-ta]-šid-du-ú-šú

o 77

[x x x x] URU.kal-ḫa

o 88

[x x x x] ⸢ÍD*⸣.ḫi-ri-te

o 99

[x x x x x x] ⸢01* me*⸣ 20

rest broken away

(Break)

Reverse
rr beginning broken away

r 1'1'

[x x x x x x x] tú

r 2'2'

[is—su-ri be]-⸢lí*⸣ i-⸢qa⸣-bi

(r 2) [Perhaps my lo]rd would say: "Why did you not write?" I have now written to the Vizier, my lord.

r 3'3'

[ma-a a]-ta-a la taš-pu-ra

r 4'4'

[ú-ma-a an]-nu-rig

r 5'5'

[a-na] LÚv.SUKKAL EN-ia

r 6'6'

[a]-sa-pa-ra

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P334174/.
